title: Python | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Python

## [Supported versions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#supported-versions "Supported versions")

The level of support for a language is defined as follows:

- Fully supported: Analysis will complete. All the language features are understood and examined.
- Supported: Most language features are understood and examined but the version includes unsupported features. Analysis might break or provide incomplete results.

Versions 3.0 to 3.13 are fully supported.

Version 2.7 is supported.

## [Supported tools and frameworks](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#supported-tools-and-frameworks "Supported tools and frameworks")

Django, FastAPI, Flask, Pandas, Numpy, PySpark, PyTorch, Tensorflow and Scikit-learn.

## [Language-specific properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#language-specific-properties "Language-specific properties")

Discover and update the Python-specific [properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/analysis-parameters/ "properties") in the project **Administration** > **General Settings** > **Languages** > **Python**.

## [Handling project python version](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#handling-project-python-version "Handling project python version")

Python code is analyzed by default as compatible with python 2 and python 3. Some issues will be automatically silenced to avoid raising False Positives. In order to get a more precise analysis you can specify the python versions your code supports via the `sonar.python.version` parameter.

Accepted format are a comma separated list of versions having the format "X.Y"

Examples:

- `sonar.python.version=2.7`
- `sonar.python.version=3.8`
- `sonar.python.version=2.7, 3.7, 3.8, 3.9`

## [Jupyter Notebooks](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#jupyter-notebooks "Jupyter Notebooks")

Jupyter Notebooks are an open document format based on JSON. They are used for all sorts of data science tasks: data cleaning and transformation, data visualization, statistical modeling, machine learning, deep learning, etc.

### [Supported versions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#supported-versions "Supported versions")

SonarQube Cloud can analyze Jupyter Notebooks nbformat.v4 and later.

### [Specific properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#specific-properties "Specific properties")

Discover and update the Jupyter Notebooks-specific [properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/analysis-parameters/ "properties") in **Administration** > **General Settings** > **Languages** > **Python** > **Jupyter Notebooks**.

### [Managing rules](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#managing-rules "Managing rules")

Jupyter Notebook rules can be enabled and disabled in your [quality profile](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/standards/managing-quality-profiles/introduction/ "quality profile").

### [Jupyter Notebooks in SonarQube for IDE for VSCode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#jupyter-notebooks-in-sonarqube-for-ide-for-vscode "Jupyter Notebooks in SonarQube for IDE for VSCode")

You can analyze your Jupyter Notebooks projects [directly in VS Code](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/ "directly in VS Code"). Note that [Connected Mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected Mode") will be ignored when working with Jupyter Notebooks (if this feature is important to you, you can [submit the idea on SonarQube Cloud's portal](https://portal.productboard.com/sonarsource/1-sonarcloud/tabs/1-under-consideration/sonarcloud/tabs/under-consideration "submit the idea on SonarQube Cloud's portal")).

### [Important notes](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#important-notes "Important notes")

- Only Python code is analyzed in Jupyter Notebooks.
- Only primary locations are shown (see [Issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/issues/introduction/ "Issues") to learn more about primary vs secondary locations).
- Analysis does not measure code duplication at this time.

## [Related pages](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/advanced-setup/languages/python/#related-pages "Related pages")

- [External Analyzer Reports](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/external-analyzer-reports/ "External Analyzer Reports") ([Pylint](https://pylint.pycqa.org/ "Pylint"), [Bandit](https://github.com/PyCQA/bandit/blob/master/README.rst "Bandit"), [Flake8](https://flake8.pycqa.org/en/latest/ "Flake8"))
- [Test coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/ "Test coverage") (the [Coverage.py tool](https://coverage.readthedocs.io/en/7.3.2/ "Coverage.py tool") provided by [Ned Batchelder](https://nedbatchelder.com/ "Ned Batchelder"), [Nose](https://nose.readthedocs.io/ "Nose"), [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/ "pytest"))
